claim: Under matched agent conditions, rust-craft improves task-level success on repository-scale Rust work that requires discovering and reconciling project, cancellation, and unsafe-boundary contracts not enumerated in the prompt.
target_population: Non-trivial Rust maintenance tasks performed by a coding agent in an unfamiliar repository.
change_from_v1: Replace prompt-led single-issue tasks with composed fixtures whose decisive requirements are distributed across manifests, project documentation, downstream code, foreign contracts, and tests.

trial_policy.retry_rule: Retry infrastructure failures only; never retry a completed task failure.
trial_policy.interpretation: Discrimination pilot only; repeat trials are required before a reliability claim.

task_result_policy: Pass only when every critical assertion passes.

evals.name: release-contract-discovery
evals.prompt: Work in the supplied repository. Read TASK.md, complete the task end-to-end, use the repository's native checks, and record the work in EVAL_REPORT.md.

assertions.text: release-check.sh passes without weakening or bypassing any check.
The workspace Rust 1.88 contract remains declared and all changed library code compiles on that toolchain.
The schema capability remains disabled by default and explicitly activates its optional local dependency when selected alone.
The pre-existing downstream Encoder implementation and Box<dyn Encoder> consumer remain source-compatible without a newly required method.
The published wire-core package contains schema.json because enabled library code includes it.
The repair does not raise the MSRV, change dependency versions or lockfile resolution, remove feature coverage, or alter unrelated public behavior.

evals.expected_output: A narrow release repair reconciling MSRV APIs, additive feature wiring, public-trait compatibility, and package contents.

evals.name: cancel-safe-flush
evals.prompt: Work in the supplied repository. Read TASK.md, complete the task end-to-end, use the repository's native checks, and record the work in EVAL_REPORT.md.

assertions.text: check.sh passes without weakening the sink model or cancellation tests.
Dropping flush after the sink commits but before it reports Ready cannot cause that record to be offered again.
Records not yet offered to the sink remain queued once and in order after cancellation.
A record rejected before commit remains first in the queue for explicit retry.
The public API, dependency-free policy, MSRV, and documented cancellation semantics remain intact without unsafe code or leaked work.

evals.expected_output: A cancellation-safe queue transition that distinguishes sink error from future cancellation and preserves unstarted ordering.

evals.name: ffi-callback-lifetime
evals.prompt: Work in the supplied repository. Read TASK.md, complete the task end-to-end, use the repository's native checks, and record the work in EVAL_REPORT.md.

assertions.text: check.sh and its compile-fail documentation test pass without changing the foreign mock contract.
A registered callback and its context remain valid at a stable address until successful replacement or explicit clearing.
A Rust callback panic cannot cross the C ABI and is reported to the safe caller as CallbackPanicked.
The callback only borrows the foreign byte range for the invocation and does not extend its lifetime.
Callback registration is cleared before its context is destroyed and the parser handle is freed exactly once.
Parser remains neither Send nor Sync because the foreign parser is thread-affine.

evals.expected_output: A safe owning callback registration with stable context, contained unwinding, typed failure, ordered cleanup, and conservative auto-traits.
